item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 3636's response to 'danger! high voltage' was the clincher for me. why change what is already perfect, indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went from liking this to loving it as soon as the SCP's personality became more present

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test 3 was funny, but the one that really got me laughing was Test 6. Nice work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incredibly happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you made me change my definition of perfection into "Danger! High Voltage" by Electric Six, i hope you are fucking happy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also
Leave it all behind you - Cult To Fallow
the first 5 words are literally "Suffocate everything and complicate everything"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a well written SCP! It's got the perfect amount of testing logs and it made me laugh multiple times. I like how you wrote the jukebox with a tiny bit of personality, it made it more enjoyable to read :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for featuring this on the front page, it was great to log in and see my article pop right up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wish there were more songs this idea is great. I would love to see what my favorite songs video would be. +1

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded: While I was reading the article, my mind was scouring the lyrics of every song I knew to see which would be most chaotic. I ended settling on "Troubled Times" by Michael Drange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear god, please no… but…

Test XX
Song selected: I Ejaculate Fire - Dethklok
SCP-3631-1 Event: All biological human males within a 500m radius will begin to experience immense sexual stimulation, leading to an orgasm. Once that occurs, magma instantaneously appears, which seems to come from the crotch region.
Effect: Subjects in radius during event will most likely die due to the extremely hot temperature of the magma.
Notes: When this song is played in november, no event takes place, for unknown reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Here comes the sun" by the Beatles would probably be on the blacklist too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wonderwall - 'no I dont like that song'
I actually laughed at this, thank you and take this +1

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded: Reading about SCP-3636 really brought a smile to my face. You should be very proud of your beautiful addition to the SCP universe. Well done!

item.title: Mod Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Please don't respond to your own posts. Click options -> edit and edit your original post rather than creating addition responses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind, just found it in the testing log in a much superior form.

item.title: Mod Post - Info - Close
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I cannot believe I have to say this, but do not make school shooting jokes.

Christ Alive.

Do not respond to this post unless you are staff. Responding to it may result in disc action

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happen to "Never Gonna Give You Up" by Rick Asley? Does the machine refuse to rick roll someone, or does it retaliate? (I think it should be violent on this song to contrast it's happy tune/message of love)

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded: A school shooting happens twice that day in america, as opposed to the regular one a day.

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded: What if you played Pumped Up Kicks by Foster the People?

item.title: Why is ring of fire not ya know
item.content:encoded: Why is ring of fire not blacklisted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hell yeah, I love extended expirement logs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hilarious read once the recovery was listed.
+1

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded:
un-blacklist it's raining men

🎶 💃 🎶 Hallelujah 🎶 💃 🎶

That song is stuck in my head now, I hope you're happy

item.title: Re: SCP-3636
item.content:encoded: You should un-blacklist it's raining men. For obvious reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was really well done. The effect of "Jukebox Hero" literally made me lol. And I loved how it has taste inmusjc so it won't play certain songs! The God-like power it has works for me because the skip uses it very sparingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blacklisted song: Black Hole Sun

oh dear jesus that's bad.

…Would an instance SCP-1981-J happen if you played "don't stop believing"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was thinking about that or if not for the sake of cross-testing being pretty much baned with 682, what about super soldiers?like a NTF especialized super OP force or something, how about making a cover version of 7 nation army to serve the fundation?
Also cant think of a pun funy enoughto be their squad name but sugestions are welcome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I have to put up my preemtive suggestion for the test log. They should try playing an original song composed by a Foundation staff member about SCP-682 being permanently destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please let this be on the test log. I need this in my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're killin' me here!

But. More honest feedback is that this is just a great skip overall, and the list of banned songs serves as a unique way of invoking the otherwise well-trodden implied horror.

And implied comedy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't believe that anybody feels the way I do, about this comment.

There are many things I'd like to say to you, but I don't know how (the discussion page works).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've been on this site since September, right? That's nine months to learn how things work around here. I honestly expected better — by now, you should've somehow realized what you've got to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because it correctly identifies Wonderwall as trash.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder why it doesn't like Wonderwall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this in draft form! I'm glad to see you fixed the problem of the test logs. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two muscular Slavic men are seen trying to escape together, eventually they stop searching for a method of escape and stand embracing each other and kissing as the boat sinks.

werk

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A friend and I were thinking, and we figured nothing should ever be played on here for the sake of anything. We originally thought, maybe "We are the world" could be a good one to play on it, but then we thought of everyone turning into planets and children.

Maybe Pornstar Dancing by My Darkest Days wouldn't be too bad No. Bad Zacky. Bad.

Needless to say, lock that bitch up keep it safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this on the forums. Glad to see it on the mainsite now! Have a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wasn't invasive. Moreso "I feel like the author is smart enough to figure that out themselves". You're fine here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sorry if it seemed invasive, was just saying just in case. sorry Zyn. I was hopped up on the idea to add Alien Kids - Alien Rap to this SCP because I think it's a funny idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aren't you allowed to make one?

You are allowed to make your own.

pastarasta1, just because the author has not stated that they want to make a collab right this minute doesn't mean they don't know it's allowed. I'm pretty sure they already understand that they can make a collab log for their own article since a couple people have already recommended doing so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd veer away from a blacklist collab log

Agreed, I was just throwing out some amusing ones that popped into my head. X3
It's perfect like it is, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pssst, Pumped up Kicks is about school shootings, but i see what you did there. Very creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well. Changed it to Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok yeah, collaborative blacklist log would suck now that I think about it, much better to make a test log then a simple blacklist anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Collaborative blacklist log would be absolute trite, I agree. Extremely easy to make low-quality stuff in there, and harder to come up with good original ones. I just think a log with testing would suffice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe if there was quality control by the author or others?

never mind thought we where talking about the regular one not the blacklist one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd veer away from a blacklist collab log - it seems like it'd end up as a lower-effort dumping ground, and ripe for repetitive entries (how many flipping 'may cause the end of the world' songs do we really need? That being said, I am 100% down for a collab testing log; to continue from where snipes88 left off:

Test # X
Song selected: Pumped up Kicks - Foster the People
SCP-3631-1 Event: Several dozen muscular humanoid legs manifested within 1km of the testing area. Instances assaulted personnel to the beat of the song, resulting in minor bruising and several cases of shattered testicals.
Effects: Instances expired at the songs conclusion. Testing revealed them to be non-anomalous, all were found to be genetically identical to Mark Foster.
Notes: Could be worse, at least it wasn't a group of SCP-1993 - Researcher Davis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: aren't you allowed to make one? just make a post and write a few tests and post it for others to add in? i mean, 261's testing log was inserted by Dr. Gears. You are allowed to make your own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would absolutely love a supplementary test log that is open for collaboration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's completely fine to make a collaborative testing log no matter what time it is, if it's either right when you post or a month later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
^ please do this also we need a supplementary blacklist one as well because there are a lot to be blacklisted.

seriously author do it, i need to dance to a happy alien beat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This begs for a supplementary test log, open for collaboration. However, you'll need to set some rules for it (especially in-universe), because some of the test logs in this article alone stretch belief for "why would the Foundation fucking test with this song of all songs". Also, this is absolutely Euclid.

That said, I love this article. Greatjob!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also anything made by Murzbow because not only is it loud who knows what would happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about danger music? Or John Cage's 4'33''? I love this. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another one we don't need this thing playing: Pumped up Kicks - Foster the People

that would just end horribly for everyone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gonna third Euclid, based on

Addendum: On ██/██/17, SCP-3636 began playing "Why Can't We Be Friends" by War without any input. No staff were on hand to observe the video event corresponding to the song. Security forces entered the room after noticing the song and discovered former Foundation employee ████ ███████ handcuffed inside the containment room. ███████ was wanted by the Foundation for the theft of six anomalous items. The stolen items were later found to have been returned to their proper locations. A note was found in ███████'s pocket. The note read "Please keep the music playing, I'd rather be friends than enemies. - WGJB" Testing resumed by order of O5.

This shows that it can and will act on it's own to do something if left alone. Something that can potentially lead to someone being dragged to where they don't belong, say a civilian.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More for the blacklist:
Thriller - Michael Jackson
Eye In the Sky - The Alan Parsons Project
London Calling - The Clash
The Final Countdown - Europe
The Ride of the Valkyries - Richard Wagner
Firestorm - Far Too Loud
Doomsday - Nero
Flaming Telepaths - Blue Öyster Cult
The entirety of the collaborative concept album Destroid by Excision, et al.
Kids With Guns - Gorillaz
The Safety Dance - Men Without Hats
Battle Without Honor Or Humanity - Tomoyasu Hotei
Radioactive - Imagine Dragons

ETA: Should the collaborative log be added, dibs on "For What Its Worth" by Buffalo Springfield. Just had an interesting idea for that one.
